Join me from <hide> am to <hide> pm at Fire Om Earth, <hide> Mill Hollow Rd., Eureka Springs, Arkansas, for a one-day work: Backyard Medicinals: Into the Wild with Steven Foster.
This is the only Arkansas event I have scheduled for <hide>!
For details go to Fire Om Earth's workshop page.
Early bird registration deadline extended until April <hide>th.
$<hide>.<hide>.
After April <hide>th, $<hide>.<hide>.
Also checkout Melissa Clare's Plant Spirit Communications event, and Lorna Trigg's Creating a Medicine Wheel Garden at Fire Om Earth, April <hide>th.
Make a weekend of it.
The Ozarks have nearly <hide> species of plants, which includes about <hide>% that are edible and medicinal, perhaps <hide> species.
It is helpful to understand that different plants occur in different habitats.
We will look at which plants you might find in a specific habitat.
Spring is our best season for wild edibles and medicinal plants, the time when we get dandelion greens, wild onions, pepper grass, plantain, pokeweed, and purslane, among others and a good time of year to enjoy edible flowers such as violets and elderflowers.
Come prepared to learn, enjoy, and ask questions.
Japanese honeysuckle, to some a weed, to me a medicinal plant.

In segments of the conservation community the question has arisen as to whether the possibility exists that some plants considered weeds in the United States may have some kind of economic value in terms of medicinal or food use that could translate into a commercial harvest, and hence an opportunity to control the weeds.
Japanese honeysuckle produces two major ingredients in Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM).
The flowers are known as jin yin hua which consists of the dried, unopened buds of Japanese honeysuckle.
Another product is the dried stems with leaves attached known as ren dong teng.
The Chinese name jin yin hua means gold and silver flower.
This refers to the fact that the white flowers turn golden yellow one or two days after blooming.
The Chinese name, referring to the stems with the leaves attached, ren dong, means "stand in winter" in reference to the fact that the leaves are evergreen.
more info.......well come to the workshop!!!

Close on the heels of Prime Minister Sh Narendra Modi's Swachh Bharat Abhiyaan, the Gian Sagar College and Hospital scrubbed clean village Fatehpur Garhi on outskirts of Rajpura under its Swasth Bharat Abhiyaan.Chairman and Senior Oncologist Dr. Barjinder Singh of the institute said that the keeping in view the unhygienic conditions prevalent in village,s the college decided to adopt this village, involve its people and clean the village that has population of <hide> people.
"The village comes under village adoption scheme of the Gian Sagar Medical College and Hospital.
Under this a team of eight doctors of various specialties visited door-to-door for the OPD's of the villagers residing in the village.
The referred patients were provided free bus service, along with the discount on their procedures and investigations and as well as pharmacy, which extended up to <hide> discounts," he said.
Having completed the free medical services as a part of its adoption scheme, the team of Gian Sagar Medical College and Hospital thereafter took up the gargantuan task of cleaning and tidying up the village.
General Manager, Marketing Natasha Chopra added when team reached the village, surroundings and sewer system was in pathetic state.

"Village's pits were full of polybags, waste lay strewn and there were bushes along the road.
The drains overflowed with sewer water, which formed puddles on road.
In absence of proper bins for dumping garbage, the waste was strewn around in the village," she said.
Teams were pided and places were allotted, so that the teams can work efficiently and effectively.
For this a team eleven members were sent to the village.
After all the formalities and authorities taken from the sarpanch of the village, the team started its two days task of cleaning the whole village from one corner to another.Time to time villagers were given knowledge about cleanliness, about cleaning the places, keeping the roads clean.
A meeting was organized with sarpanch in order to clarify people about importance of maintaining hygiene.

Lectures on waste management shall be organized from time to time for the villagers as a part of orientation.Along with the villagers, team from Gian Sagar Medical College worked hard, devoting their time and drained off their sweat Drains were cleaned, big piles of garbage were taken off to the places where all the garbage was thrown Gian Sagar shall also be providing garbage bins to the village, so that the villagers throw all the trash into the bins.
He added that instiute aims at adopting more villages in similar way.

The Artadia Awards <hide> Los Angeles Awardees are: Cayetano Ferrer, Nicole Miller, Vishal Jugdeo, Stanya Kahn and Kerry Tribe.
For two consecutive days (November <hide>-<hide>, <hide>), three internationally known jurors: Ali Subotnick, Curator, The Hammer Museum, University of Los Angeles, California; Magnolia de la Garza, Associate Curator, Museo Rufino Tamayo, Mexico City; and Joao Ribas, most recently of MIT's List Visual Art Center-conducted <hide>-minute studio visits with each of the <hide> short-listed artists.
The Finalists were chosen from over <hide> visual art applicants living and working in the Los Angeles County.
The two recipients of the <hide> Artadia Los Angeles Awards at the $<hide> level are: Cayetano Ferrer and Nicole Miller.
The three recipients of the $<hide> awards are: Vishal Jugdeo, Stanya Kahn and Kerry Tribe.
This is Artadia's first award cycle in Los Angeles.
Since <hide>, Artadia has recognized artistic excellence is cities across the United States with unrestricted, merit-based awards to contemporary artists chosen through a rigorous jury process involving a panel of internationally recognized curators.

In addition to Los Angeles, Artadia currently funds awards on a rotating cycle in Atlanta, Boston, Chicago, Houston, and San Francisco.
Applications for the Artadia Awards were open to visual artists in all media and at any stage of their career working and living in the Los Angeles County area.
The application was available online for three months from July <hide> to September <hide>, <hide>.
The three jurors: Ali Subotnick; Chus Martinez, Chief Curator of El Museo del Barrio, New York; and Spencer Finch, Artist, New York-named the ten Finalists in New York in October.
Artadia will present an exhibition of the winning artists work in Los Angeles in January <hide>.
To honor the generous gifts of the Artadia Los Angeles Council, Cayetano Ferrer and Kerry Tribe were named Los Angeles Council Awardees.
